feed.title: Using AI tooling for code review

entry.title: Using AI tooling for code review
entry.summary:
The concept of utilizing an artificial intelligence like Claude or ChatGPT to facilitate an automatic peer review club is intriguing and shows potential for enhancing the efficiency of reviewing processes. The idea revolves around the possibility of setting up a repository of background information and prompts within a markdown (.md) file hosted on GitHub. This setup would enable AI technologies such as Claude or ChatGPT to offer round-the-clock availability for peer review tasks, without necessitating any local software configuration. Such an approach could significantly streamline the peer review process, making it more accessible and possibly even available through a free tier, thereby reducing barriers to entry for users.
The enthusiasm for this innovative quiz idea stems from its perceived ease of implementation once the initial setup hurdles are overcome. However, the originator of the idea has been preoccupied with other projects, delaying experimentation with this concept. The notion of leveraging AI in this manner suggests a forward-thinking approach to problem-solving and project management, highlighting the ongoing search for tools that can automate and enhance traditional workflows. This proposal not only reflects an interest in exploiting cutting-edge technology but also hints at the untapped potential of AI in simplifying complex tasks, such as peer reviews, which are essential components of collaborative work environments and educational settings.
